name: Las Fuentecillas and Los Callejones Route
name_original: Ruta de las Fuentecillas y los Callejones – trail run circular por el Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca
description: A difficult 8.2-mile jogging route in Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park, featuring unique karstic formations at Los Callejones de Las Majadas.
region_intro: Jogging around Las Majadas offers diverse terrain within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park, characterized by its unique karst formations and extensive pine forests. The region features mountainous landscapes at an average altitude of over 1,300 meters, with dramatic ravines and gorges carved into the limestone. Jogging routes traverse shaded forest paths, open mountainous areas, and natural "alleys" formed by ancient geological wonders. Best jogging routes around Las Majadas The most popular jogging route is Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – trail run circular from Uña via the Escalerón path to La Raya, a 6.0 miles (9.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 25 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers varied terrain through the natural park. Another top favourite among local runners is Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – running circular to Laguna de Uña, a moderate 2.1 miles (3.4 km) path. This route provides scenic views around the Laguna de Uña. Local runners also love the Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – circular by Los Callejones de Las Majadas, a 2.3 miles (3.7 km) trail leading through unique karst formations, often completed in about 27 minutes. Jogging around Las Majadas is defined by extensive pine forests, dramatic ravines, and unique karst formations like Los Callejones. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging trails. The routes in Las Majadas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 130 runners have used komoot to explore Las Majadas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running routes around Las Majadas?
A: Jogging around Las Majadas offers a diverse range of terrain within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park. You'll encounter unique karst formations, extensive pine forests, and mountainous landscapes at an average altitude of over 1,300 meters. Routes traverse shaded forest paths, open mountainous areas, and natural 'alleys' formed by ancient geological wonders. The region is also characterized by dramatic ravines and gorges.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Las Majadas?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014398409/parque-natural-de-la-serrania-de-cuenca-circular-by-los-callejones-de-las-majadas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – circular by Los Callejones de Las Majadas&lt;/a&gt; is rated as easy, covering 2.3 miles (3.7 km) and typically completed in about 27 minutes. This route takes you through fascinating karst formations.

Q: What are some of the natural attractions or landmarks I can see while jogging in Las Majadas?
A: The region is rich in natural wonders. You can explore the unique karst formations of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4396615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Callejones of Las Majadas&lt;/a&gt;, which resemble a labyrinthine stone maze. The nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enchanted City&lt;/a&gt; also features stunning rock formations. For breathtaking views, consider routes that pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3797272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventano del Diablo&lt;/a&gt;, offering spectacular vistas of the Júcar River canyon, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3790692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal de la Tola Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4737494" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uña Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; is another beautiful spot.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available in the area?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Las Majadas are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014406304/parque-natural-de-la-serrania-de-cuenca-trail-run-circular-from-una-via-the-escaleron-path-to-la-raya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – trail run circular from Uña via the Escalerón path to La Raya&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014404502/parque-natural-de-la-serrania-de-cuenca-running-circular-to-laguna-de-una" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – running circular to Laguna de Uña&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Las Majadas?
A: The landscape of Las Majadas transforms with each season, offering a different experience. While winters can be cold, summers are mild, making it pleasant for running. Spring is particularly beautiful, with trails adorned with wildflowers, wild roses, and aromatic scents from lavender and thyme. Autumn also brings vibrant colors to the extensive forests.

Q: Can I expect to see any wildlife during my run?
A: Absolutely. The natural environment of Las Majadas is a sanctuary for diverse wildlife. You might spot deer, fallow deer, roe deer, wild boars, ibex, and mouflon. Birdwatchers can also enjoy seeing griffon vultures, golden eagles, goshawks, and sparrowhawks soaring above the mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, there are several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014406304/parque-natural-de-la-serrania-de-cuenca-trail-run-circular-from-una-via-the-escaleron-path-to-la-raya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – trail run circular from Uña via the Escalerón path to La Raya&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 6.0 miles (9.7 km) trail with significant elevation changes. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014395025/las-fuentecillas-and-los-callejones-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Fuentecillas and Los Callejones Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.2 miles (13.2 km) long and also rated difficult.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Las Majadas?
A: The running routes in Las Majadas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, the unique karst formations, and the extensive pine forests that provide both challenge and scenic beauty. Over 130 runners have used komoot to explore the area's trails.

Q: Are the running routes in Las Majadas suitable for families?
A: Yes, there are routes that families can enjoy. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014398409/parque-natural-de-la-serrania-de-cuenca-circular-by-los-callejones-de-las-majadas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de la Serranía de Cuenca – circular by Los Callejones de Las Majadas&lt;/a&gt; is an easy, shorter route that offers a fascinating experience through natural rock formations, making it a great option for families with children.

Q: What kind of forests will I run through in Las Majadas?
A: The region is characterized by extensive pine forests, primarily consisting of black pine, Scots pine, savin junipers, and common junipers. In shadier ravines, you might also find maples, hollies, and lindens. These forests provide varied terrain, from shaded paths to more open mountainous routes, and contribute to the rich biodiversity of the area.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the dramatic ravines and gorges?
A: Yes, the region is known for its dramatic ravines and gorges. Routes that pass near the Júcar River canyon, such as those leading to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3797272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ventano del Diablo&lt;/a&gt;, will offer spectacular views of these natural features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Raya&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6503577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Escalerón&lt;/a&gt; trails are also located in areas with impressive geological formations.
tips: The Tío Cogote Viewpoint is one of the most spectacular viewpoints that can be found in the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Las Fuentecillas and Los Callejones Route?
faqs.answer: This route is approximately 13.2 kilometers (8.2 miles) long and generally takes about 1 hour and 45 minutes to complete at a jogging pace. Keep in mind that varied terrain and elevation changes might extend this time.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level and terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is officially classified as 'difficult' for jogging. While it includes comfortable paths and forest tracks, you'll also encounter steeper, more rugged sections and stony stretches. A notable part involves ascending a steep path up the Picón de Royo Frío, requiring careful footing.

faqs.question: Is the Las Fuentecillas and Los Callejones Route a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is a circular trail, meaning you'll end up back at your starting point in the town of Las Majadas without having to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and natural features along the route?
faqs.answer: The most significant feature is Los Callejones de Las Majadas, a geological wonder with unique karstic rock formations resembling an 'enchanted city' of labyrinthine passages and arches. You'll also pass by the Fuente de La Fuentecilla and enjoy panoramic views from the Tío Cogote Viewpoint, where you might spot griffon vultures. The trail winds through dense oak and pine forests, offering diverse scenery.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: To fully enjoy the natural beauty and avoid extreme weather, spring and autumn are generally the best times. The vegetation is lush, and temperatures are more moderate. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder conditions or snow, especially at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Las Fuentecillas and Los Callejones Route?
faqs.answer: As the route is located within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park, dogs are generally allowed but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Always practice responsible pet ownership.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the trail or the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the trails within the Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park, including this route. However, it's always a good idea to check local regulations before your visit, especially for group activities or specific areas.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and what should I know about parking?
faqs.answer: The route officially starts from the town of Las Majadas. You can usually find parking available within or near the town, which serves as the trailhead for this circular route. Arriving earlier in the day, especially on weekends, is advisable to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, while you'll primarily follow the Ruta de Las Fuentecillas y Los Callejones (PR-CU 28), sections of the route also intersect with or run alongside other paths such as the Camino de la Fuente del Rey and the Sendero de Los Callejones de las Majadas.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I see along the trail?
faqs.answer: The Serranía de Cuenca Natural Park is rich in biodiversity. Keep an eye out for various bird species, including griffon vultures soaring above the rocky cliffs. You might also spot deer, wild boar, or smaller mammals, especially during quieter times of the day.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a jog on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the varied and sometimes challenging terrain, we recommend sturdy trail running shoes, sufficient water, snacks, and appropriate clothing layers. A map or GPS device is also advisable, as well as sun protection. Be prepared for changes in weather, especially in the mountains.
